skill.name: youtube-api-skill
skill.description: Extract structured YouTube video metrics and channel information via the BrowserAct API using keyword and date filters.
skill.summary: Automate YouTube data extraction with keyword search and upload date filtering through BrowserAct.
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: The skill makes legitimate HTTPS requests to the documented BrowserAct API and reads the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable. No malicious intent was found. Static scanner flags for external_commands and weak cryptography were false positives triggered by markdown backtick syntax in SKILL.md. The script uses standard library requests with hardcoded API endpoints and passes user-provided keywords only as safe JSON payload fields.

low_findings.title: External API dependency
low_findings.description: The script sends user-provided keywords to a third-party service (BrowserAct). Users must trust that the service handles data responsibly.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script explicitly transmits keywords and an API key to browseract.com. This is expected behavior for the skill, but it creates a dependency on an external service and potential data exposure.

low_findings.title: Environment variable dependency for API key
low_findings.description: The script reads BROWSERACT_API_KEY from the environment. If the host environment is shared or misconfigured, the key could be exposed to other processes.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: os.getenv is a standard and safe pattern, but in multi-tenant or shared environments environment variables can leak. This is a standard operational risk rather than a code vulnerability.

content.value_statement: Manually collecting YouTube video and channel data is slow and error-prone. This skill automates structured extraction of views, likes, comments, and subscriber counts via the BrowserAct API.
actual_capabilities: Search YouTube by keywords and upload date filters
Extract structured video metrics including views, likes, and comments
Retrieve channel information such as subscriber counts and URLs
Poll long-running BrowserAct tasks and return final results
Handle API errors and guide users to provide missing credentials
limitations: Requires a valid BrowserAct API key configured in the environment
Dependent on BrowserAct service availability and task queue times
Only supports the data fields returned by the BrowserAct template
May take several minutes for complex search queries

use_cases.title: Competitor Channel Monitoring
use_cases.description: Track recent video performance, views, and engagement metrics for competitor channels on a weekly basis.

use_cases.title: Content Trend Research
use_cases.description: Discover high-performing videos in a niche by filtering uploads from the past week and analyzing engagement data.

use_cases.title: Campaign Performance Tracking
use_cases.description: Extract detailed metrics for promotional videos to evaluate reach, likes, and comments across campaigns.

prompt_templates.title: Basic keyword search
prompt_templates.prompt: Find YouTube videos about 'AI automation' uploaded this week and give me the views and likes.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner user looking for quick metrics on a topic

prompt_templates.title: Competitor scan
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for videos from competitor channels about 'project management software' uploaded this month. I need channel names, subscriber counts, video URLs, and view counts.

prompt_templates.title: Niche trend analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract metrics for the top 20 videos about 'no-code tools' uploaded this week. Include video duration, view count, like count, and comment count so I can compare engagement rates.

prompt_templates.title: Automated reporting pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a weekly report that searches for 'Claude Code tutorials' and outputs a structured table with channel title, video title, publish date, views, likes, and comments. Save the results for my BI dashboard.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user integrating data into a reporting workflow

output_examples.input: Get YouTube metrics for 'OpenAI GPT-5' videos uploaded this week.
output_examples.output: Channel: TechInsights | Subscribers: 245K | Video: GPT-5 Leaks and Rumors | URL: youtube.com/watch?v=abc123 | Published: 2 days ago | Views: 89,432 | Likes: 4,210 | Comments: 312 | Duration: 12:34

output_examples.input: Compare competitor videos about 'browser automation' from this month.
output: 1. AutoBot Channel (120K subs) - 'Browser Automation 101' - 45K views, 1.2K likes, 89 comments
2. DevTools Daily (80K subs) - 'Automate with Playwright' - 32K views, 890 likes, 45 comments
3. CodeStream (200K subs) - 'Selenium vs Playwright' - 78K views, 3.4K likes, 210 comments

best_practices: Always verify that the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable is set before running searches
Use specific keywords and narrow date ranges to reduce task execution time
Review the output for completeness before using it in reports or dashboards
anti_patterns: Do not share your BrowserAct API key in chat or commit it to version control
Do not assume the API will return all videos; results depend on BrowserAct template limits
Do not retry indefinitely on failure; follow the one-retry limit to avoid rate issues

faq.question: What is the BrowserAct API and why do I need a key?
faq.answer: BrowserAct provides automated browser workflows that extract data from websites. A valid API key authenticates your requests and is required to run the YouTube extraction template.

faq.question: How do I get a BrowserAct API key?
faq.answer: Visit the BrowserAct Console integrations page, sign in, and copy your API key. Set it as the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable before using this skill.

faq.question: How long does a typical extraction task take?
faq.answer: Tasks usually take a few minutes because they involve real browser automation. The script prints status logs with timestamps so you can monitor progress.

faq.question: What data fields are returned for each video?
faq.answer: The skill returns channel title, channel URL, subscribers, video title, video URL, publish date, view count, video duration, comment count, and like count.

faq.question: Can I filter results by upload date?
faq.answer: Yes. You can filter by Today, This week, This month, or This year. The default filter is This week.

faq.question: What should I do if the task fails?
faq.answer: If the output contains Invalid authorization, check your API key. For other errors, the skill will retry once automatically. If it fails again, report the error message.
